title: Xeraea sonorae
intro: Xeraea sonorae, also known as Mexican prickly poppy or Prickly Poppy, is a plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including pain relief, reduction of inflammation, and antimicrobial activity. Bioactive constituents of Xeraea sonorae include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The plant is commonly used in traditional herbal medicine, with preparations including teas, infusions, and tinctures that are used to treat various conditions, such as arthritis, wounds, and skin irritations.
taxonomy: Xeraea sonorae is classified in the family Sapotaceae, a group of flowering plants that includes over 150 species of trees and shrubs commonly known as sapotes. The genus Xeraea is a monotypic genus, containing only one species, Xeraea sonorae. This plant is native to the Sonoran Desert in the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Xeraea sonorae is an evergreen shrub that produces small white flowers and edible fruits. The classification of Xeraea sonorae has been confirmed through botanical studies and is widely accepted by taxonomists.
